description: Search ranking: intent-bearing queries (create/update/delete/list/detail) rank the correct endpoint method/shape at top
Search ranking: relevant entries returned, clearly-irrelevant entries excluded; placeholder-metadata entries penalized
Request-building: registry entry + args -> correct method + path with path params substituted
Request-building: query/body placed correctly; GET classified read (auto), POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE classified mutation
Dual-edition registry generation: CE spec yields CE registry, EE spec yields EE registry (EE is a superset; EE-only endpoints absent from CE)
Regression: existing EE chat assistant search->call tool flow behaves unchanged after re-point onto agent-tooling
meta/mcp-registry endpoint returns edition-correct registry and requires auth (401 without a valid API key)
Connector startup: missing env vars fail fast with a clear message; registry fetch failure reported clearly
call_api_endpoint dispatches to /api/v1 with the user token and returns the parsed result
API 4xx/5xx (incl. 401) surfaces as a structured tool error, not a crash
MCP protocol conformance (stdio): tools/list returns exactly the 3 meta-tools; tools/call invokes each successfully
E2E smoke: configured connector lists and reads a ticket from a real instance under the user's permissions
MCP protocol conformance (Streamable HTTP): single endpoint serves tools/list + tools/call for the 3 meta-tools
OAuth: unauthenticated MCP request rejected; protected-resource metadata advertised; auth-code+PKCE yields a token that authorizes calls
Agent authz: kernel enforces agent subject — allowed tool/resource permitted, denied blocked
Agent authz: a mutation is blocked when the agent lacks permission (reads still allowed)
Audit completeness: every tool invocation writes an audit row with required fields; export returns them
Agent ABAC: read-only-on-billing policy blocks billing mutations while allowing billing reads
Approval queue: gated mutation enqueues; approve executes it; reject blocks it; timeout expires it
Quotas: per-agent rate limit is enforced once the threshold is exceeded
